documentedClaim.statement: Same-name: enterprise > personal > project > bundled. Plugin skills are namespaced. Nested variants remain directory-qualified.
documentedClaim.citation: docs/ontology/README.md §6.1 (Claude Code Skills row), citing https://code.claude.com/docs/en/skills

entries.reason: docs/ontology/README.md §6.1 documents a specific, ordered precedence chain for this exact collision -- personal (user) over project -- citing official docs. That IS existing, sourced evidence (tier 2 in docs/knowledge/README.md §6's evidence priority), but it is a claim this PR-06 qualification pass did not itself behaviorally confirm: no safe non-interactive path exists to dump Claude Code's resolved skill catalog without a real session (claude -p executes a real model turn and is excluded by the safety boundary; --safe-mode disables customizations rather than reporting on them). Per issue #10's explicit instruction not to let 'cover these three cases' pressure produce a guessed winner, selectedSource is recorded UNKNOWN and the documented claim is kept in the separate documentedClaim field rather than promoted to selectedSource. mergeOperator REPLACE is this fixture's best-fit mapping of the documented 'X > Y > Z > W' precedence chain onto docs/ontology/README.md §3.1's closed operator vocabulary (a strict override chain, not a namespace-preserving coexistence like Codex's documented Skill behavior) -- that mapping is itself unconfirmed, not an additional fact.
